Category: Parent-subsidiary buy-in TOB (completing a multi-stage control arc)
Industry: Sportswear / apparel (Descente, Le Coq Sportif, Munsingwear, Umbro)

Unaffected Price (local): ¥3,420 (2024/06/26 close, per Second Proposal premium calc)

Recurring Attempt by Same Acquirer?: Yes — Itochu's SECOND transaction with Descente: 2019 hostile partial TOB to 40% (JP-078), then this 2024 full privatization. Itochu began acquiring Descente shares in 1971; largest shareholder since the 1980s
Toehold / Irrevocables / Tender Agreemen: BS Investment held 44.46% pre-deal (as of 2024/03/31); min tender set to reach 2/3 of voting rights

Final Resolution Mechanism: TOB 85.92% → EGM 2024/12/25 → share consolidation squeeze-out → delisted TSE Prime 2025/01/24; Descente now wholly-owned Itochu subsidiary
Notes / Follow-ups: NEW DEAL 2026/07/12 (owner-approved). LANDMARK C3: completes the Itochu/Descente arc — the 2019 hostile partial TOB (JP-078, first successful hostile TOB involving major public cos) established 40% veto control and replaced the founder-grandson president; this 2024 transaction converts that control into full ownership. Notable SC conduct: rejected ¥3,800, demanded ¥4,600, extracted ¥4,350 (+14.5% over the second proposal). Sibling/parent node: JP-078.

The SC then extracted a +35% increase from ITOCHU's lowball ¥9,611 opening proposal (which offered only a 3.34% premium) to ¥13,000. Category: Parent divestiture via partial TOB to a foreign strategic (novel counter-TOB carve-out)
Conclusion / Status: In progress / concluded 2025 — TOB result and exact completion date TBV
Industry: Batteries & electronic devices (battery packs, energy storage, next-gen batteries)
Deal Size (local, JPY bn unless noted): TBV (¥435 × up to 15,527,400 sh ≈ ¥6.8bn — BELOW the $100mn scope floor; retained for precedent value, see Notes)
Deal Structure: PARTIAL tender offer at ¥435/share with BOTH a maximum (15,527,400 sh / 45.00%) and a minimum limit; Fujitsu (58.82% parent) agreed to tender ALL its shares but Silitech will only purchase up to the 45% cap — so Fujitsu exits control while RETAINING a residual stake and a brand-license relationship; FDK deconsolidated from Fujitsu
Initial Offer / Price Bumps: Final proposal ¥425/share @ 40% max (2024/12/20) → Fujitsu rejected on total-proceeds grounds (2024/12/27) → amended ¥425 @ 45% max (2025/01/10) → final ¥435 @ 45% (1 price bump + 1 size bump — Fujitsu negotiated on TOTAL consideration, not just per-share)
Board Recommendation: For (FDK board expressed opinion in favour; noted brand license from Fujitsu continues and business will proceed without particular changes)
Competing Bid / Interloper: No — but the Tender Offer Agreement contained an explicit COUNTER-TOB carve-out permitting Fujitsu to accept a superior competing bid (defined as ≥3% higher on total-consideration basis, made ≥5 business days before the TOB deadline)
Toehold / Irrevocables / Tender Agreemen: Fujitsu 58.82% (20,295,422 sh) — Tender Offer Agreement commits Fujitsu to tender ALL shares, but the 45% purchase cap means Fujitsu retains a residual stake post-deal and remains a major shareholder
Rules / Regulations Triggering Review: FIEA TOB (partial offer with max & min limits); inbound foreign acquisition of a Japanese listed subsidiary (FEFTA screening likely — classification TBV)
Key Debate Points / Frictions: Fujitsu's portfolio-transformation programme (same strategic driver as Shinko Electric, JP-010, and Fujitsu General, JP-056); the deal was sourced via PSA Group intermediation with SMBC Nikko as Fujitsu's FA; the negotiation is instructive — Fujitsu pushed back not on PRICE PER SHARE but on TOTAL PROCEEDS, extracting a larger purchase cap (40%→45%) rather than a higher price, then a modest ¥425→¥435 bump. The contractual counter-TOB carve-out is a notable market-check mechanism.
Final Resolution Mechanism: Partial TOB; FDK deconsolidated from Fujitsu; FDK remains listed (no squeeze-out/delisting — this is a control transfer, not a take-private)
Verification Status: Verified via primary sources (new-deal pass 2026/07/12); TOB result, completion date, premium, and FEFTA classification all TBV — needs a follow-up pass
Notes / Follow-ups: NEW DEAL 2026/07/12 (owner-approved, 'Fujitsu-adjacent'). BELOW the >US$100mn scope floor (≈¥6.8bn / ~$45mn purchase) — RETAINED under the precedent-value override (Japan_Landmark_Criteria.md §3) for three reasons: (1) novel PARTIAL-TOB-with-cap structure where the parent tenders 100% but can only sell 45%, deliberately leaving a residual stake + brand license; (2) explicit CONTRACTUAL COUNTER-TOB carve-out (≥3% superior, ≥5 bd before deadline) as a built-in market check — a mechanism worth tracking; (3) completes the FUJITSU PORTFOLIO-TRANSFORMATION FAMILY with Shinko Electric (JP-010, ¥685bn to JIC) and Fujitsu General (JP-056, ¥256bn to Paloma Rheem) — three different structures for shedding three listed subsidiaries. Inbound Taiwanese strategic acquirer (cf. Yageo/Shibaura JP-044). FOLLOW-UP: TOB result, completion date, unaffected price/premium, FEFTA classification.
Filing / First Report Date: TBV
Min Tender Condition Detail: Both a MINIMUM and a MAXIMUM limit set; maximum 15,527,400 shares (45.00% shareholding ratio) — the cap is the structurally decisive term, since Fujitsu tenders 58.82% but only 45% can be purchased
Financial Advisor (Target): SMBC Nikko Securities Inc. — FA to FUJITSU (the selling parent), not to FDK itself; FDK-side FA TBV
